findings.detail: Single straight flowchart LR chain A-->B-->C-->D-->E, 5 nodes, no branches/side-spurs — matches stated intent exactly. Governing concept is in the config `title:` field, not a dangling ghost node. All 5 labels (Accept Classroom Link, Personal Repo Created, Edit File in Browser, Commit with Message, Commit History View) are ≤4 words and fuzzy-match corpus sections 5.2-5.4 (accept-assignment flow, Classroom auto-provisioning, browser edit/commit, commits/history view). No overlaps, no truncation, no crossings (none possible on a single rank), no edge labels to collide.

Color tier usage (start/act=blue, auto=purple, hitl=yellow, done=green) reads as a consistent stage-type palette rather than a mix-up — D is the one node requiring a human-authored decision (the commit message), plausibly justifying the hitl/yellow tier versus B's fully-automated Classroom provisioning (auto/purple). Not flagged as a violation.

stats.mermaid_parse: not independently re-run (no bash tool available in this invocation); PNG structure (title, all 5 node labels/subtitles, all 5 classDef colors, single A-->B-->C-->D-->E chain) matches diagram.mmd source exactly, which is strong evidence the existing render is current and parsed without error.
